content.problemStatement:
Implement a First-In-First-Out (FIFO) queue using two stacks. The implemented queue should support the following operations: push, pop, peek, and isEmpty.



Implement the StackQueue class:



void push(int x): Adds element x to the end of the queue.


int pop(): Removes and returns the front element of the queue.


int peek(): Returns the front element of the queue without removing it.


boolean isEmpty(): Returns true if the queue is empty, false otherwise.
constraints: The queue will not contain more than 1000 elements at any time
content.inputFormat: The input will contain a list of commands and a list of inputs. The commands will be one of the following: push, pop, peek, isEmpty. The inputs will be a list of integers.
content.outputFormat: The output will be a list of results for each command. For push, the result will be null. For pop and peek, the result will be the integer value. For isEmpty, the result will be a boolean value.

hidden.label: tricky case / empty-like / negative if allowed

solution.approach: Use two stacks to implement the queue. The first stack will be used to store the elements in the order they are pushed. The second stack will be used to store the elements in the reverse order. When the pop or peek operation is performed, the elements will be transferred from the first stack to the second stack if the second stack is empty.
solution.timeComplexity: O(n) for push, pop, and peek operations in the worst case
solution.spaceComplexity: O(n) for storing the elements in the two stacks
